Application Summary

OneIM VB.NET Auditor

A PowerShell-based static analysis and live telemetry skill for One Identity Manager VB.NET code — covering security, correctness, performance, reliability, and concurrency.

Target platform: One Identity Manager 9.3.1
Rules: 50 active
Test coverage: 319 passing
Example files: 100 .vb files (50 rules × 2)
Engine: PowerShell 5.1 + 7
Generated: 2026-03-27
Overview· At a Glance· Static Audit· Rule Catalog· Rule Authoring· Schema Snapshot· Benchmarking· Cascade Report· Reference Library· Reporting· Test Suite· Script Index

🔍Overview

The OneIM VB.NET Auditor is a structured skill that performs automated code review of One Identity Manager VB.NET scripts, templates, methods, formatting rules, and process-related code. It combines a static analysis engine (pattern matching + coded detectors) with live database telemetry (schema snapshots, benchmarks, cascade dependency graphs) to surface quality problems with traceable evidence.

It is intentionally scoped to OneIM-specific VB.NET patterns — not generic VB.NET desktop or web applications — and enforces an approved evidence hierarchy (One Identity docs → Microsoft Learn → OWASP) so every finding can be justified to an auditor or a CTO.

Audit priority order is fixed: (1) Correctness → (2) Security → (3) Performance. All findings are classified under one of five categories: security, correctness, reliability, performance, or concurrency.

📊At a Glance

🛡️
Security Rules
12
SEC100 – SEC190
✅
Correctness Rules
9
VB001–VB005, OIM510, OIM511, REL210, REL220
🔗
Reliability Rules
7
REL200–REL250, OIM545
⚡
Performance Rules
13
PER400–PER455, OIM500, OIM520, OIM555
⚙️
Concurrency Rules
7
CNC600–CNC610
🧪
Passing Tests
319
4 Pester lanes, 0 failures

📁Core Functionality — Static Audit

The primary function: scan one or more VB.NET files or folders and produce a structured report of findings. This works entirely offline with no live OneIM connection required.

powershell -File scripts/invoke_oneim_audit.ps1 `
  -TargetPath   "C:\MyProject\Scripts" `
  -ReferenceRepoPath "C:\ReferenceLib\oneim-9.3.1" `
  [-BenchmarkReportPath output\benchmark-report.json] `
  [-SchemaSnapshotPath  output\schema-snapshot.json] `
  [-ThresholdsFile      thresholds.json] `
  [-CascadeReportPath   output\cascade-Person.FirstName.json] `
  [-SuppressionsFile    suppressions.json]
  • 📂
    Recursive file scanningWalks all .vb files in the target folder; each file is processed through all active detection units
  • 🎯
    50 active rulesTwo engines: coded (PowerShell dispatch table) and declarative (line-pattern, header-required, line-absence)
  • 🔕
    SuppressionsRule + path glob + justification; suppressed findings never appear in the report
  • 📏
    max_examples_per_file capPrevents a single noisy rule from flooding the report for one file; cap is per-rule in the catalog
  • 🔁
    Finding deduplicationCoded + declarative double-reports on the same rule/file/line are merged before output
  • 🌐
    Benchmark ingestionWhen a benchmark report and thresholds file are supplied, threshold overruns become additional PERF findings
  • 🔗
    Cascade ingestionWrite-cycle nodes from a cascade JSON become CNC610 findings in the audit report
  • 📝
    Path scopingRules can be restricted via include_paths/exclude_paths globs to specific folders

📋Rule Catalog — All 50 Rules

Rules live in assets/audit-rules.json and are the single source of truth. Two detector engines are used: coded (PowerShell function dispatched per file line) and declarative (regex/pattern interpreted at runtime — no code change needed to add).

IDTitleCategorySeverityEngine
VB001Option Explicit should be enabledcorrectnessmediumcoded
VB002Option Strict should be enabledcorrectnessmediumcoded
VB003Avoid obvious late binding and Object-heavy codecorrectnessmediumcoded
VB004Use Is Nothing / IsNot Nothing instead of = Nothing / <> Nothingcorrectnessmediumcoded
VB005Do not rethrow with Throw ex — bare Throw preserves the stack tracecorrectnessmediumdeclarative
SEC100Avoid string-built SQL or filter expressionssecurityhighcoded
SEC110Avoid launching external processes from OneIM script logicsecurityhighcoded
SEC120Broad exception handling should not hide failuressecuritymediumcoded
SEC130Avoid obsolete web request APIssecuritymediumcoded
SEC131Review outbound web requests with variable-driven URLs (SSRF)securitymediumcoded
SEC140Review file I/O paths that appear to be variable-drivensecuritymediumcoded
SEC150Review XML parsing for insecure DTD or external entity behavior (XXE)securitymediumcoded
SEC160Do not use insecure deserializers (BinaryFormatter, NetDataContractSerializer)securityhighcoded
SEC170Review deprecated TLS or disabled certificate validationsecurityhighcoded
SEC180Hardcoded credential or secret literalsecurityhighdeclarative
SEC185Password embedded in a connection string literalsecurityhighdeclarative
SEC190LDAP filter built by string concatenation — injection risksecurityhighcoded
REL200Dispose disposable objects before losing scopereliabilitymediumcoded
REL210Use explicit StringComparison for identifier-sensitive comparisonsreliabilitymediumcoded
REL220Review GetTriggerValue-driven writes for Nothing or empty handlingreliabilitymediumcoded
REL230Empty Catch block silently swallows exceptionsreliabilitymediumcoded
REL240GetFk result used without IsEmpty guardreliabilitymediumcoded
REL250IDisposable resource declared with Dim instead of Using — resource may leakreliabilitymediumdeclarative
OIM500Review PutValue / ExecuteTemplate usage for sync-time side effectscorrectnesslowcoded
OIM510Custom VI-KEY entries should use CCC-prefixed script namescorrectnesslowcoded
OIM511Custom VI-KEY entries should implement CCC-prefixed Function or Sub namescorrectnesslowcoded
OIM520Expensive OneIM lookup (GetListFor / GetSingleProperty) inside a loopperformancemediumcoded
OIM530Use f.UidComparison() for UID_ columns instead of f.Comparison(…, ValType.String)correctnessmediumcoded
OIM535Direct = "" comparison on .GetValue("UID_…").String — use String.IsNullOrEmptycorrectnesslowdeclarative
OIM545GetSingleValue() throws when no row matches — prefer TryGetSingleValue()reliabilitymediumdeclarative
OIM555IEntity.Save(Session) called inside a loop — use UnitOfWork.Put() for bulk writesperformancemediumcoded
PER400Prefer Any over Count for emptiness checksperformancelowcoded
PER410Avoid repeated string concatenation in loopsperformancelowcoded
PER420Review possible multiple enumeration of IEnumerableperformancelowcoded
PER430Review repeated object or database access inside loopsperformancelowcoded
PER440Object allocation inside a loop body (GC pressure)performancelowcoded
PER450Connection pooling disabled (Pooling=false) in ADO.NET connection stringperformancemediumdeclarative
PER451IEntity.Create(Session) called inside a loop — one SELECT per itemperformancemediumcoded
PER452Query.SelectAll() loads every column — prefer Select() with needed columns onlyperformancelowdeclarative
PER455BuildSessionFactory() called per method invocation — factory should be cachedperformancemediumcoded
STR600Avoid .ToLower()/.ToUpper() for string comparison — use StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCasecorrectnesslowcoded
STR610String.Format() called with a variable as the format string — format injection risksecuritymediumdeclarative
WEB600Use HttpClient instead of obsolete HttpWebRequest / WebClient (SYSLIB0014)reliabilitymediumdeclarative
CNC600NOLOCK hint may yield dirty reads under concurrent writesconcurrencyhighdeclarative
CNC601BeginTransaction without visible Commit or Rollback in scopeconcurrencyhighcoded
CNC602Possible nested transaction — BeginTransaction called more than once before Commitconcurrencyhighcoded
CNC603Thread.Sleep blocks a OneIM sync threadconcurrencymediumdeclarative
CNC604Unbounded loop (While True / Do While True)concurrencymediumdeclarative
CNC605Shared (static) mutable field risks race conditions across concurrent sync threadsconcurrencymediumdeclarative
CNC610Template write (PutValue/ExecuteTemplate) inside a dependency cycleconcurrencyhighcoded

✏️Rule Authoring Workflow

New declarative rules can be added without touching detector PowerShell code. Three declarative match kinds are supported:

Match KindFires WhenFinding Location
line-patternRegex matches any line in the fileEach matching line
header-requiredRegex is absent from the first 40 linesLine 1
line-absenceNo line in the entire file matches the regexLine 1

Add a new rule in 4 steps

  1. Scaffold with new_audit_rule.ps1Appends a draft declarative rule to audit-rules.json; validates the regex before writing; rejects duplicate IDs
  2. Validate with validate_audit_rules.ps1Checks unique IDs, valid enums, regex compilation, required fields per engine type, and optional source-ID resolution
  3. Promote from draft → activeChange the "state" field in audit-rules.json; the rule immediately becomes part of the active audit run
  4. Run the test suitescripts/run_auditor_tests.ps1 must still pass 319 tests with 0 failures

Path scoping: add include_paths or exclude_paths glob arrays to any rule to restrict it to specific relative paths (e.g., ["Scripts\\*"]). Uses PowerShell WildcardPattern — case-insensitive.

🗄️Live Schema Snapshot (Lane 2)

Connects to the live OneIM database and exports table/column/FK-relation metadata for FK-aware analysis and cascade resolution. Works in three access modes.

scripts/export_oneim_schema_snapshot.ps1
  [-TestAssistRoot <path>]  [-ProjectConfigPath <path>]
  [-AccessMode Auto|Session|Direct]
  [-OutputDir <path>]  [-Force]
  • 📦
    Output: schema-snapshot.jsonTables, columns, FK relations, plus pre-built indexes: column_to_fk_target and parent_to_children for O(1) lookups
  • 🔄
    Smart cachingExisting snapshot is reused on subsequent runs; -Force regenerates even if a recent snapshot exists
  • 🔀
    Three access modesAuto (tries Direct first, falls back to Session), Session (TestAssist QConsole), Direct (ADO.NET SqlConnection)
  • 📊
    Schema diffcompare_schema_snapshots.ps1 diffs two snapshots and writes a schema-diff.json; exit 1 when -FailOnDiff is set

⏱️Benchmarking (Lane 3)

Measures the runtime performance of OneIM templates, script methods, and/or processes. A built-in sampler captures elapsed time, memory delta, CPU delta, GC collection counts per generation, and thread delta — no external tooling required. Optionally uses BenchmarkDotNet if available.

scripts/invoke_oneim_benchmark.ps1
  -TemplateNames Person.FirstName, Person.LastName
  [-ScriptMethodNames <name>]  [-ProcessNames <name>]
  [-WarmupCount 1]  [-MeasuredRunCount 10]
  [-ThresholdsFile thresholds.json]
  [-SchemaSnapshotPath <path>]
  [-BaselineBenchmarkPath <path>]  [-RegressionThreshold 20]
  [-CollectSqlMetrics]

Metrics computed per target

MetricDescription
avg_ms / stddev_ms / min_ms / max_msCore elapsed-time statistics
p50_ms / p75_ms / p95_ms / p99_msPercentile latency (P95 is the primary threshold metric)
cv_pct / stabilityCoefficient of variation; labelled stable / variable / unstable
warmup_effect_ratioRatio of first-sample cost to mean — detects JIT warm-up cost
total_gc_gen0/1/2Cumulative GC collection counts across measured runs
max_thread_deltaPeak thread-count increase during measurement
regression_pct / regressedPercentage change vs. a baseline; flagged when above -RegressionThreshold

🕸️Cascade Report (Template Dependency Graph)

Builds a directed dependency graph for a single root template, tracing how field computations flow through FK relations, script method calls, and successor templates. Detects cycles using Kosaraju's two-pass SCC algorithm.

scripts/export_template_cascade_report.ps1
  -TemplateName Person.FirstName
  [-SchemaSnapshotPath <path>]  [-TemplateCatalogPath <path>]
  [-BenchmarkReportPath <path>]  [-ThresholdsFile <path>]
  • 📈
    Predecessor + successor treesCollapsible HTML sections showing all upstream and downstream template dependencies
  • 🔴
    Bottleneck highlightingNode with the highest avg_elapsed_ms highlighted in red when benchmark data is present
  • 🟡
    Cycle detectionCycle nodes and edges shown in amber; explicit cycle path arrays in the JSON companion
  • 🟠
    Threshold overrunsNodes exceeding configured max_avg_ms or max_p95_ms shown in red when -ThresholdsFile is supplied
  • ⚠️
    Write-cycle risk (CNC610)SCCs where any member is a writer are classified as concurrency_risk = write-cycle and fed back into the audit as CNC610 findings
  • 🗺️
    Topological order + depth histogramJSON output includes topological_order and depth_histogram for build-order analysis

📚Reference Library Management

The auditor requires a curated reference library (not a raw System Debugger export) to validate findings against. A set of scripts manages the full lifecycle from raw export to promoted custom reference.

  • 📥
    register_reference_export.ps1Registers a raw export as mixed-export; seeds candidate-custom from extracted custom snippets
  • ✂️
    extract_custom_reference_files.ps1Extracts custom-marked VB snippets from a raw System Debugger export
  • 🧹
    materialize_sanitized_ootb_export.ps1Creates a heuristic OOTB baseline by removing CCC custom ranges from a mixed export
  • 📋
    export_candidate_review_plan.ps1Generates a snippet-by-snippet JSON + Markdown review plan for a candidate entry
  • ✅
    promote_custom_reference.ps1Promotes a reviewed candidate into promoted-custom when confidence meets the threshold
  • ❌
    reject_custom_reference.ps1Moves a candidate into rejected with a recorded rationale and manifest trail

📄Reporting

The audit produces a self-contained HTML report with interactive features. No external assets or CDN dependencies.

  • 📊
    Executive summaryTotal findings, severity histogram, and category breakdown table
  • 📁
    File-grouped findingsEach finding group is headed by its relative path; collapsible with one click
  • 🔘
    Severity filter checkboxesHigh / Medium / Low — hiding a severity also hides groups that become empty
  • 🖨️
    Print CSSFilter controls hidden; findings formatted for clean paper output
  • 🔗
    File index navigationJump-to links for each file group when the report covers more than one file
  • 📤
    Rule set exportexport_audit_rules.ps1 writes audit-rules.md, .json, and .csv as a Bundle; supports -RuleState [All|Active|Draft|Disabled] filter

Finding detail fields

Every finding includes: rule ID, title, category, severity, confidence, file path, line number, triggering scenario, remediation, test ideas, and source IDs (mapped to approved-sources.json).

🧪Test Suite

Pester 4.10.1 regression suite run by scripts/run_auditor_tests.ps1 in four independent lanes:

● PowerShell 5.1

Lane 1 — Core Auditor

138 tests covering reference validation, all 50 detection rules (positive + negative), post-processing, suppression, deduplication, HTML report features, rule export, schema diff, rule validation, rule scaffolding, and end-to-end audit runner.

● PowerShell 7

Lane 2 — Schema Snapshot

19 tests covering the schema snapshot module: table/column/FK parsing, index building, environment fingerprinting, and cache reuse logic.

● PowerShell 7

Lane 3 — Benchmark

52 tests covering Get-SampleStats (percentiles, stability labels, warmup ratio, GC totals, thread delta), Get-ThresholdResult, Get-RegressionResult, and Get-OneImAuditDmvDelta.

● PowerShell 7

Lane 4 — Graph / Cascade

110 tests covering the graph module: node/edge management, DFS, Kosaraju SCC, topological sort (Kahn's), depth histogram, is_writer flag, write-cycle concurrency risk classification, and idempotency.

🗂️Script & Module Index

Entry-point scripts

invoke_oneim_audit.ps1Main audit runner
export_oneim_schema_snapshot.ps1Database schema capture
invoke_oneim_benchmark.ps1Template / method / process benchmarking
export_template_cascade_report.ps1Cascade dependency graph + HTML
compare_schema_snapshots.ps1Two-snapshot diff
export_audit_rules.ps1Export rule catalog as Markdown / JSON / CSV / Bundle
validate_audit_rules.ps1Structural validation of audit-rules.json
new_audit_rule.ps1Scaffold a new declarative rule
run_auditor_tests.ps1Pester regression suite (4 lanes)

Reference library scripts

register_reference_export.ps1Bootstrap a raw export into the managed library
extract_custom_reference_files.ps1Extract CCC custom snippets
materialize_sanitized_ootb_export.ps1Create OOTB baseline
export_candidate_review_plan.ps1Snippet review planning
review_custom_reference.ps1Update confidence scoring
split_candidate_reference.ps1Split review-plan bands
promote_custom_reference.ps1Promote to promoted-custom
reject_custom_reference.ps1Move to rejected with rationale

PowerShell modules

OneimAudit.Common.psm1Shared path, directory, and confidence helpers
OneimAudit.RuleCatalog.psm1Rule loading, state filtering, export, finding creation
OneimAudit.Reference.psm1Reference path validation and bootstrap logic
OneimAudit.Detectors.psm1All 50 detection units (coded + declarative dispatch)
OneimAudit.PostProcessing.psm1Suppression, deduplication, example-cap enforcement
OneimAudit.Reporting.psm1HTML report rendering with filters, file groups, print CSS
OneimAudit.LiveSession.psm1TestAssist session bootstrap, SQL query helper (Session / Direct / Auto)
OneimAudit.Graph.psm111 graph functions: nodes, edges, DFS, Kosaraju SCC, topological sort, depth histogram
OneimAudit.ConcurrencyDetectors.psm1Concurrency-specific detection (CNC601, CNC602, CNC610 context)

Assets

assets/audit-rules.json50 active rules — single source of truth for all rule metadata
assets/approved-sources.jsonMachine-readable source catalog; all rule source_ids must resolve here
assets/suppressions.example.jsonSuppression format template (rule_id + path_glob + justification)
examples/<RULE_ID>/violation.vb100 example files — violation + compliant pair for every rule
OneIM VB.NET Auditor — oneim-vbnet-auditor skill  ·  Version 9.3.1 target  ·  50 rules  ·  319 tests  ·  Report generated 2026-03-27
